title: What They’re Saying: Experts and Impacted Communities Support City of Yes for Carbon Neutrality Initiative
description: NEW YORK – The City Planning Commission today held a public hearing on City of Yes for Carbon Neutrality, the first of Mayor Adams’ three “City of Yes” zoning proposals. City of Yes for Carbon Neutrality is a citywide zoning amendment that would modernize the city’s Zoning Resolution to facilitate climate action, clean energy, and resiliency by removing barriers to greener energy, buildings, transportation, and water and waste systems.
commentary_en: The City of Yes for Carbon Neutrality initiative aims to modernize zoning regulations, which can significantly impact real estate development and infrastructure projects in New York City. By facilitating cleaner energy and more sustainable building practices, this initiative could lead to increased investment in green infrastructure and eco-friendly developments.
reason_en: This news item is relevant because it addresses a citywide zoning amendment that will remove barriers to sustainable development. Such changes can lead to increased investment in real estate projects that prioritize climate resilience and energy efficiency, potentially reshaping the local market and attracting new investments in green technologies and infrastructure.
signals: Support from experts and communities for climate action
Potential for increased investment in green infrastructure
neighborhood_catalog: Jacob Riis Park-Fort Tilden-Breezy Point Tip
